name: Minecraft 3D Structure Generation
description: Generative deep learning pipeline for 3D voxel world generation. Uses a custom Variational Autoencoder (VAE) with residual vector quantization and a latent diffusion prior to generate 32x32x32 voxel structures with 3,717 block types, compressed into a discrete 8x8x8 latent representation with 96%+ codebook utilization.
keyDecisions: Used a dual-head decoder separating volume prediction and block-type classification, improving reconstruction accuracy from 21.6% to 66.9% after 10 architecture iterations
Implemented a Straight-Through Estimator to handle training instability with discrete latent codes
Fixed a LayerNorm inverse transform bug in the generation pipeline, recovering 47.5pp of accuracy without retraining
Chose residual vector quantization over standard VQ-VAE for better codebook utilization

name: Multi-Agent Research Debate System
description: AI agent framework for structured research decision-making. A multi-agent LLM debate system using 11 Claude Code native agents across three epistemological roles with a supervisor orchestrating 2-round debates, post-debate falsification, and overconfidence calibration.
keyDecisions: Used 11 agents with three distinct epistemological roles to create genuine debate rather than echo-chamber consensus
Built a research retrieval pipeline aggregating findings from 15+ academic surveys to mitigate LLM training data cutoff limitations
Implemented caching to reduce repeat query latency to <100ms
Integrated arXiv and Semantic Scholar APIs with PDF parsing and citation graph traversal for evidence injection into agent prompts

name: Rainier Route
description: A public transit and biking integration app for the Seattle Metropolitan area. Multi-modal routing engine that generates and scores 5 parallel route combinations (bike-only, transit-only, and 3 bike-to-transit variants) using Haversine distance optimization for nearest-station selection.
keyDecisions: Used Haversine distance optimization for nearest-station selection with weighted duration scoring, reducing estimated commute times by up to 20%
Built a Node.js REST API orchestrating parallel requests across Google Maps Platform and GTFS-Realtime feeds from King County Metro and Sound Transit
Docker-containerized deployment with graceful transit fallback, achieving under 500ms per route calculation
React frontend with dynamic polyline rendering and distinct bike/transit color coding, achieving 4.3/5 usability rating

name: Syncy
description: Cross-platform social music and universal playlist sharing app. Full-stack playlist conversion platform integrating 4 music streaming services (Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, SpotAPI) via OAuth 2.0 PKCE, MusicKit JS, and device code flows.
keyDecisions: Used ISRC codes for cross-platform track matching with a fuzzy search fallback (artist + title + duration tolerance) for tracks without ISRC
Built a TrackMapping cache layer to minimize redundant API calls during playlist conversion
Implemented OAuth 2.0 PKCE and device code flows with encrypted token storage and auto-refresh across 4 different streaming service auth patterns
Designed a platform-agnostic playlist import system with batch processing, duplicate prevention, and JSONB track metadata storage across 5 database models

name: Emergency Copilot
description: AI-powered CCTV threat detection and dispatcher alert platform. Real-time surveillance system with a two-stage detection pipeline: Overshoot SDK for rapid 1-second clip analysis, then Google Gemini 2.5-flash for extended AI incident timelines with state-change tracking and confidence scoring.
keyDecisions: Two-stage detection pipeline: Overshoot SDK streaming mode for rapid threat detection in 1-second clips, then batched snapshots (3-10 per 10-second window) fed into Gemini for detailed incident analysis
WebSocket snapshot ingestion with SSE broadcast to dispatcher dashboards for real-time alert propagation
Haversine-based geospatial clustering to group CCTV incidents within 50-meter radius and 1-hour time window automatically
LiveKit WebRTC for sub-second latency video streaming to dispatchers
Built with Bun + Express.js API, PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM, full TypeScript across 4 database tables and 7 custom React hooks
